codeReview.summary: All three DRY violations were cleanly resolved. The asArray utility was extracted to helpers.js and both duplicate definitions removed from ModuleDetail.js. All 10 named URL constants were extracted to constants.js with clear category organization and JSDoc header. All 4 consuming files (App.js, WelcomeDialog.js, AboutTab.js, VideoCard.js) now import from constants with zero hardcoded URLs remaining. The inline spinner styles (8 CSS properties) plus the container's padding/text-align were migrated to two CSS classes in core.css (.lazy-loading and .lazy-loading-spinner). Only the dynamic progress bar width remains as inline style in App.js. Handoff verification confirms all 426 tests pass, lint is clean (0 errors, 4 pre-existing warnings), format check passes, and build succeeds.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill's CSS migration pattern example only shows a .lazy-loading-spinner class, but the worker also moved the container's padding and text-align to a .lazy-loading class. This was correct and thorough behavior, but the skill example could be updated to show both classes for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 2 CSS Migration section only mentions '.lazy-loading-spinner' class. Worker also created '.lazy-loading' class in core.css for the container div's padding/text-align. core.css lines 537-552.

summary: Clean, thorough implementation of all three DRY violations. The asArray extraction, URL constants extraction, and CSS migration are all correct and well-organized. All validation contract assertions (VAL-DRY-001 through VAL-DRY-003, VAL-CROSS-001 through VAL-CROSS-005) are satisfied. No bugs, edge cases, or issues found.
